ROMEO enters.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: Here comes Romeo, here comes Romeo.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Without his roe, like a dried herring. O flesh, flesh,
how art thou fishified! Now is he for the numbers that
Petrarch flowed in. Laura to his lady was but a
kitchen-wench— marry, she had a better love to berhyme
her— Dido a dowdy, Cleopatra a gypsy, Helen and Hero
hildings and harlots, Thisbe a grey eye or so, but not
to the purpose. — Signior Romeo, bonjour! There’s a
French salutation to your French slop. You gave us the
counterfeit fairly last night.

French salutation to your French slop. You gave us the
counterfeit fairly last night.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Without his roe, like a dried herring. O flesh, flesh,
how art thou fishified! Now is he for the numbers that
Petrarch flowed in. Laura to his lady was but a
kitchen-wench— marry, she had a better love to berhyme
her— Dido a dowdy, Cleopatra a gypsy, Helen and Hero
hildings and harlots, Thisbe a grey eye or so, but not
to the purpose. — Signior Romeo, bonjour! There’s a
French salutation to your French slop. You gave us the
counterfeit fairly last night.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Good morrow to you both. What counterfeit did I give
you?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Good morrow to you both. What counterfeit did I give
you?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: The slip, sir, the slip. Can you not conceive?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: The slip, sir, the slip. Can you not conceive?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Pardon, good Mercutio, my business was great, and in
such a case as mine a man may strain courtesy.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Pardon, good Mercutio, my business was great, and in
such a case as mine a man may strain courtesy.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
That’s as much as to say, such a case as yours
constrains a man to bow in the hams.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Meaning “to curtsy”?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Thou hast most kindly hit it.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: A most courteous exposition.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Nay, I am the very pink of courtesy.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Pink for flower.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Right.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Why, then is my pump well flowered.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Sure wit, follow me this jest now till thou hast worn
out thy pump, that when the single sole of it is worn,
the jest may remain, after the wearing solely singular.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Sure wit, follow me this jest now till thou hast worn
out thy pump, that when the single sole of it is worn,
the jest may remain, after the wearing solely singular.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
O single-soled jest, solely singular for the
singleness.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Come between us, good Benvolio. My wits faints.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Come between us, good Benvolio. My wits faints.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Switch and spurs, switch and spurs, or I’ll cry a
match.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Nay, if our wits run the wild-goose chase, I am done,
for thou hast more of the wild-goose in one of thy wits
than, I am sure, I have in my whole five. Was I with you
there for the goose?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Nay, if our wits run the wild-goose chase, I am done,
for thou hast more of the wild-goose in one of thy wits
than, I am sure, I have in my whole five. Was I with you
there for the goose?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Nay, if our wits run the wild-goose chase, I am done,
for thou hast more of the wild-goose in one of thy wits
than, I am sure, I have in my whole five. Was I with you
there for the goose?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Thou wast never with me for anything when thou wast not
there for the goose.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: I will bite thee by the ear for that jest.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Nay, good goose, bite not.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Thy wit is a very bitter sweeting. It is a most sharp
sauce.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Thy wit is a very bitter sweeting. It is a most sharp
sauce.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: And is it not well served into a sweet goose?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Oh, here’s a wit of cheveril, that stretches from an
inch narrow to an ell broad!

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
I stretch it out for that word “broad,” which, added
to the goose, proves thee far and wide a broad goose.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Why, is not this better now than groaning for love? Now
art thou sociable. Now art thou Romeo. Now art thou
what thou art—by art as well as by nature, for this
driveling love is like a great natural that runs lolling
up and down to hide his bauble in a hole.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:

art thou sociable. Now art thou Romeo. Now art thou
what thou art—by art as well as by nature, for this

up and down to hide his bauble in a hole.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: Stop there, stop there.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Thou desirest me to stop in my tale against the hair.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: Thou wouldst else have made thy tale large.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Oh, thou art deceived. I would have made it short, for
I was come to the whole depth of my tale, and meant,
indeed, to occupy the argument no longer.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Oh, thou art deceived. I would have made it short, for
I was come to the whole depth of my tale, and meant,
indeed, to occupy the argument no longer.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Oh, thou art deceived. I would have made it short, for
I was come to the whole depth of my tale, and meant,
indeed, to occupy the argument no longer.
The NURSE enters with her servant, PETER.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Here’s goodly gear.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: A sail, a sail!

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Two, two—a shirt and a smock.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Peter!

sentences.speaker: PETER
sentences.text: Anon!

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: My fan, Peter.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Good, Peter, to hide her face, for her fan’s the fairer
face.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Good, Peter, to hide her face, for her fan’s the fairer
face.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: God ye good morrow, gentlemen.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: God ye good e’en, fair gentlewoman.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Is it good e’en?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
‘Tis no less, I tell you, for the bawdy hand of the
dial is now upon the prick of noon.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
‘Tis no less, I tell you, for the bawdy hand of the
dial is now upon the prick of noon.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Out upon you! What a man are you?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Out upon you! What a man are you?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: One, gentlewoman, that God hath made, himself to mar.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
By my troth, it is well said. “For himself to mar,”
quoth he? Gentlemen, can any of you tell me where I may
find the young Romeo?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
By my troth, it is well said. “For himself to mar,”
quoth he? Gentlemen, can any of you tell me where I may
find the young Romeo?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
I can tell you, but young Romeo will be older when you
have found him than he was when you sought him. I am the
youngest of that name, for fault of a worse.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
I can tell you, but young Romeo will be older when you
have found him than he was when you sought him. I am the
youngest of that name, for fault of a worse.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: You say well.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Yea, is the worst well? Very well took, i’ faith,
wisely, wisely.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Yea, is the worst well? Very well took, i’ faith,
wisely, wisely.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: If you be he, sir, I desire some confidence with you.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: She will indite him to some supper.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: A bawd, a bawd, a bawd! So ho!

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: A bawd, a bawd, a bawd! So ho!

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: A bawd, a bawd, a bawd! So ho!

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: A bawd, a bawd, a bawd! So ho!

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: What hast thou found?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
No hare, sir, unless a hare, sir, in a Lenten pie—that
is, something stale and hoar ere it be spent.
[Sings]
An old hare hoar,
And an old hare hoar,
Is very good meat in Lent.
But a hare that is hoar
Is too much for a score
When it hoars ere it be spent.
[He speaks] Romeo, will you come to your father’s?
We’ll to dinner, thither.

[He speaks] Romeo, will you come to your father’s?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: I will follow you.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Farewell, ancient lady. Farewell, lady, lady, lady.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Farewell, ancient lady. Farewell, lady, lady, lady.
BENVOLIO and MERCUTIO exit.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
I pray you, sir, what saucy merchant was this that was
so full of his ropery?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
A gentleman, Nurse, that loves to hear himself talk,
and will speak more in a minute than he will stand to in
a month.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
A gentleman, Nurse, that loves to hear himself talk,
and will speak more in a minute than he will stand to in
a month.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
An he speak any thing against me, I’ll take him down,
an he were lustier than he is, and twenty such Jacks.
And if I cannot, I’ll find those that shall. Scurvy
knave! I am none of his flirt-gills. I am none of his
skains-mates. [To PETER] And thou must stand by, too,
and suffer every knave to use me at his pleasure?

and suffer every knave to use me at his pleasure?

sentences.speaker: PETER
sentences.text:
I saw no man use you at his pleasure. If I had, my
weapon should quickly have been out, I warrant you. I
dare draw as soon as another man if I see occasion in a
good quarrel and the law on my side.

sentences.speaker: PETER
sentences.text:
I saw no man use you at his pleasure. If I had, my
weapon should quickly have been out, I warrant you. I
dare draw as soon as another man if I see occasion in a
good quarrel and the law on my side.

sentences.speaker: PETER
sentences.text:
I saw no man use you at his pleasure. If I had, my
weapon should quickly have been out, I warrant you. I
dare draw as soon as another man if I see occasion in a
good quarrel and the law on my side.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Now, afore God, I am so vexed that every part about me
quivers. Scurvy knave! [To ROMEO] Pray you, sir, a word.
And as I told you, my young lady bid me inquire you
out. What she bade me say, I will keep to myself. But
first let me tell ye, if ye should lead her into a
fool’s paradise, as they say, it were a very gross kind
of behavior, as they say. For the gentlewoman is young,
and therefore, if you should deal double with her, truly
it were an ill thing to be offered to any gentlewoman,
and very weak dealing.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Nurse, commend me to thy lady and mistress. I protest
unto thee—

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Nurse, commend me to thy lady and mistress. I protest
unto thee—

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Good heart, and i’ faith, I will tell her as much.
Lord, Lord, she will be a joyful woman.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Good heart, and i’ faith, I will tell her as much.
Lord, Lord, she will be a joyful woman.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: What wilt thou tell her, Nurse? Thou dost not mark me.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: What wilt thou tell her, Nurse? Thou dost not mark me.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
I will tell her, sir, that you do protest, which, as I
take it, is a gentlemanlike offer.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Bid her devise
Some means to come to shrift this afternoon.
And there she shall at Friar Lawrence’ cell
Be shrived and married. [Gives her coins] Here is for
thy pains.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Bid her devise
Some means to come to shrift this afternoon.
And there she shall at Friar Lawrence’ cell
Be shrived and married. [Gives her coins] Here is for
thy pains.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: No, truly, sir. Not a penny.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: No, truly, sir. Not a penny.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Go to. I say you shall.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
[Takes the money] This afternoon, sir? Well, she shall
be there.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
[Takes the money] This afternoon, sir? Well, she shall
be there.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
And stay, good Nurse. Behind the abbey wall
Within this hour my man shall be with thee
And bring thee cords made like a tackled stair,
Which to the high top-gallant of my joy
Must be my convoy in the secret night.
Farewell. Be trusty, and I’ll quit thy pains.
Farewell. Commend me to thy mistress.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Now God in heaven bless thee! Hark you, sir.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Now God in heaven bless thee! Hark you, sir.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: What sayst thou, my dear Nurse?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Is your man secret? Did you ne’er hear say,
“Two may keep counsel, putting one away?”

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Is your man secret? Did you ne’er hear say,
“Two may keep counsel, putting one away?”

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Warrant thee, my man’s as true as steel.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Well, sir, my mistress is the sweetest lady.—Lord,
Lord! when ’twas a little prating thing.—Oh, there is a
nobleman in town, one Paris, that would fain lay knife
aboard, but she, good soul, had as lief see a toad, a
very toad, as see him. I anger her sometimes and tell
her that Paris is the properer man. But, I’ll warrant
you, when I say so, she looks as pale as any clout in
the versal world. Doth not rosemary and Romeo begin both
with a letter?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Ay, Nurse, what of that? Both with an R.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Ay, Nurse, what of that? Both with an R.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Ah, mocker, that’s the dog’s name. R is for the—No, I
know it begins with some other letter, and she hath the
prettiest sententious of it, of you and rosemary, that
it would do you good to hear it.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Ah, mocker, that’s the dog’s name. R is for the—No, I
know it begins with some other letter, and she hath the
prettiest sententious of it, of you and rosemary, that
it would do you good to hear it.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Commend me to thy lady.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Ay, a thousand times —Peter!

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Ay, a thousand times —Peter!

sentences.speaker: PETER
sentences.text: Anon!

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: [Giving PETER her fan] Before, and apace.
They all exit.
JULIET enters.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
The clock struck nine when I did send the Nurse.
In half an hour she promised to return.
Perchance she cannot meet him. That’s not so.
Oh, she is lame! Love’s heralds should be thoughts,
Which ten times faster glide than the sun’s beams,
Driving back shadows over louring hills.
Therefore do nimble-pinioned doves draw love
And therefore hath the wind-swift Cupid wings.
Now is the sun upon the highmost hill
Of this day’s journey, and from nine till twelve
Is three long hours, yet she is not come.
Had she affections and warm youthful blood,
She would be as swift in motion as a ball.
My words would bandy her to my sweet love,
And his to me.
But old folks, many feign as they were dead,
Unwieldy, slow, heavy, and pale as lead.
O God, she comes.—O honey Nurse, what news?
Hast thou met with him? Send thy man away .

The NURSE and PETER enter.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Peter, stay at the gate.
PETER exits.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Now, good sweet Nurse— O Lord, why look’st thou sad?
Though news be sad, yet tell them merrily.
If good, thou shamest the music of sweet news
By playing it to me with so sour a face.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
I am aweary. Give me leave awhile.
Fie, how my bones ache! What a jaunt have I!

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
I would thou hadst my bones and I thy news.
Nay, come, I pray thee, speak. Good, good Nurse, speak.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Jesu, what haste! Can you not stay awhile?
Do you not see that I am out of breath?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Jesu, what haste! Can you not stay awhile?
Do you not see that I am out of breath?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Jesu, what haste! Can you not stay awhile?
Do you not see that I am out of breath?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
How art thou out of breath when thou hast breath
To say to me that thou art out of breath?
The excuse that thou dost make in this delay
Is longer than the tale thou dost excuse.
Is thy news good, or bad? Answer to that.
Say either, and I’ll stay the circumstance.
Let me be satisfied. Is ’t good or bad?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Well, you have made a simple choice. You know not how
to choose a man. Romeo! No, not he, though his face be
better than any man’s, yet his leg excels all men’s, and
for a hand and a foot and a body, though they be not to
be talked on, yet they are past compare. He is not the
flower of courtesy, but, I’ll warrant him, as gentle as
a lamb. Go thy ways, wench. Serve God. What, have you
dined at home?

sentences.text:
Well, you have made a simple choice. You know not how
to choose a man. Romeo! No, not he, though his face be
better than any man’s, yet his leg excels all men’s, and
for a hand and a foot and a body, though they be not to
be talked on, yet they are past compare. He is not the
flower of courtesy, but, I’ll warrant him, as gentle as
a lamb. Go thy ways, wench. Serve God. What, have you
dined at home?

No, no. But all this did I know before.
What says he of our marriage? What of that?

sentences.text:

What says he of our marriage? What of that?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Lord, how my head aches! What a head have I!
It beats as it would fall in twenty pieces.
My back a’ t’ other side. Ah, my back, my back!
Beshrew your heart for sending me about,
To catch my death with jaunting up and down!

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
I’ faith, I am sorry that thou art not well.
Sweet, sweet, sweet Nurse, tell me, what says my love?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
I’ faith, I am sorry that thou art not well.
Sweet, sweet, sweet Nurse, tell me, what says my love?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Your love says, like an honest gentleman, and a
courteous, and a kind, and a handsome, and, I warrant, a
virtuous— Where is your mother?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Where is my mother? Why, she is within.
Where should she be? How oddly thou repliest!
“Your love says, like an honest gentleman,
‘Where is your mother?’”

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
O God’s lady dear,
Are you so hot? Marry, come up, I trow.
Is this the poultice for my aching bones?
Henceforward do your messages yourself.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Here’s such a coil. Come, what says Romeo?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Here’s such a coil. Come, what says Romeo?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Have you got leave to go to shrift today?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: I have.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Then hie you hence to Friar Lawrence’s cell.
There stays a husband to make you a wife.
Now comes the wanton blood up in your cheeks.
They’ll be in scarlet straight at any news.
Hie you to church. I must another way
To fetch a ladder, by the which your love
Must climb a bird’s nest soon when it is dark.
I am the drudge and toil in your delight,
But you shall bear the burden soon at night.
Go. I’ll to dinner. Hie you to the cell.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Hie to high fortune! Honest Nurse, farewell.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Hie to high fortune! Honest Nurse, farewell.
They exit.
FRIAR LAWRENCE and ROMEO enter.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
So smile the heavens upon this holy act
That after-hours with sorrow chide us not.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Amen, amen. But come what sorrow can,
It cannot countervail the exchange of joy
That one short minute gives me in her sight.
Do thou but close our hands with holy words,
Then love-devouring death do what he dare;
It is enough I may but call her mine.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
These violent delights have violent ends
And in their triumph die, like fire and powder,
Which, as they kiss, consume. The sweetest honey
Is loathsome in his own deliciousness
And in the taste confounds the appetite.
Therefore love moderately. Long love doth so.
Too swift arrives as tardy as too slow.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Good even to my ghostly confessor.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: Romeo shall thank thee, daughter, for us both.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: As much to him, else is his thanks too much.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Ah, Juliet, if the measure of thy joy
Be heaped like mine, and that thy skill be more
To blazon it, then sweeten with thy breath
This neighbor air, and let rich music’s tongue
Unfold the imagined happiness that both
Receive in either by this dear encounter.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Conceit, more rich in matter than in words,
Brags of his substance, not of ornament.
They are but beggars that can count their worth.
But my true love is grown to such excess
I cannot sum up sum of half my wealth.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Come, come with me, and we will make short work,
For, by your leaves, you shall not stay alone
Till Holy Church incorporate two in one.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Come, come with me, and we will make short work,
For, by your leaves, you shall not stay alone
Till Holy Church incorporate two in one.
They exit.
MERCUTIO, his page, and BENVOLIO enter with other men.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
I pray thee, good Mercutio, let’s retire.
The day is hot; the Capulets, abroad;
And if we meet we shall not ’scape a brawl,
For now, these hot days, is the mad blood stirring.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
I pray thee, good Mercutio, let’s retire.
The day is hot; the Capulets, abroad;
And if we meet we shall not ’scape a brawl,
For now, these hot days, is the mad blood stirring.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Thou art like one of those fellows that, when he
enters the confines of a tavern, claps me his sword upon
the table and says “God send me no need of thee!” and,
by the operation of the second cup, draws it on the
drawer when indeed there is no need.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: Am I like such a fellow?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Come, come, thou art as hot a Jack in thy mood as any
in Italy, and as soon moved to be moody, and as soon
moody to be moved.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Come, come, thou art as hot a Jack in thy mood as any
in Italy, and as soon moved to be moody, and as soon
moody to be moved.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Come, come, thou art as hot a Jack in thy mood as any
in Italy, and as soon moved to be moody, and as soon
moody to be moved.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: And what to?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Nay, an there were two such, we should have none
shortly, for one would kill the other. Thou, why, thou
wilt quarrel with a man that hath a hair more or a hair
less in his beard than thou hast. Thou wilt quarrel with
a man for cracking nuts, having no other reason but
because thou hast hazel eyes. What eye but such an eye
would spy out such a quarrel? Thy head is as full of
quarrels as an egg is full of meat, and yet thy head
hath been beaten as addle as an egg for quarreling. Thou
hast quarreled with a man for coughing in the street
because he hath wakened thy dog that hath lain asleep in
the sun. Didst thou not fall out with a tailor for
wearing his new doublet before Easter? With another, for
tying his new shoes with old ribbon? And yet thou wilt
tutor me from quarreling!

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Nay, an there were two such, we should have none
shortly, for one would kill the other. Thou, why, thou
wilt quarrel with a man that hath a hair more or a hair
less in his beard than thou hast. Thou wilt quarrel with
a man for cracking nuts, having no other reason but
because thou hast hazel eyes. What eye but such an eye
would spy out such a quarrel? Thy head is as full of
quarrels as an egg is full of meat, and yet thy head
hath been beaten as addle as an egg for quarreling. Thou
hast quarreled with a man for coughing in the street
because he hath wakened thy dog that hath lain asleep in
the sun. Didst thou not fall out with a tailor for
wearing his new doublet before Easter? With another, for
tying his new shoes with old ribbon? And yet thou wilt
tutor me from quarreling!

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
An I were so apt to quarrel as thou art, any man
should buy the fee simple of my life for an hour and a
quarter.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: The fee simple? O simple!

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: The fee simple? O simple!
TYBALT, PETRUCHIO, and some other CAPULETS enter.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: By my head, here comes the Capulets.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: By my heel, I care not.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text:
Follow me close, for I will speak to them.
Gentlemen, good e’en. A word with one of you.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text:
Follow me close, for I will speak to them.
Gentlemen, good e’en. A word with one of you.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
And but one word with one of us? Couple it with
something. Make it a word and a blow.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
And but one word with one of us? Couple it with
something. Make it a word and a blow.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
And but one word with one of us? Couple it with
something. Make it a word and a blow.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text:
You shall find me apt enough to that, sir, an you will
give me occasion.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Could you not take some occasion without giving?

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text: Mercutio, thou consort’st with Romeo.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Consort? What, dost thou make us minstrels? An thou
make minstrels of us, look to hear nothing but discords.
Here’s my fiddlestick. Here’s that shall make you
dance. Zounds, “consort”!

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
We talk here in the public haunt of men.
Either withdraw unto some private place,
And reason coldly of your grievances,
Or else depart. Here all eyes gaze on us.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Men’s eyes were made to look and let them gaze.
I will not budge for no man’s pleasure, I.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text: Well, peace be with you, sir. Here comes my man.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text: Well, peace be with you, sir. Here comes my man.
ROMEO enters.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
But I’ll be hanged, sir, if he wear your livery.
Marry, go before to field, he’ll be your follower.
Your worship in that sense may call him “man.”

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text:
Romeo, the love I bear thee can afford
No better term than this: thou art a villain.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Tybalt, the reason that I have to love thee
Doth much excuse the appertaining rage
To such a greeting. Villain am I none.
Therefore, farewell. I see thou know’st me not.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text:
Boy, this shall not excuse the injuries
That thou hast done me. Therefore turn and draw.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text:
Boy, this shall not excuse the injuries
That thou hast done me. Therefore turn and draw.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
I do protest I never injured thee,
But love thee better than thou canst devise,
Till thou shalt know the reason of my love.
And so, good Capulet—which name I tender
As dearly as my own—be satisfied.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
O calm dishonourable, vile submission!
Alla stoccata carries it away. [Draws his sword]
Tybalt, you ratcatcher, will you walk?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO

O calm dishonourable, vile submission!

Tybalt, you ratcatcher, will you walk?

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text: What wouldst thou have with me?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Good King of Cats, nothing but one of your nine lives,
that I mean to make bold withal, and, as you shall use
me hereafter, dry-beat the rest of the eight. Will you
pluck your sword out of his pilcher by the ears? Make
haste, lest mine be about your ears ere it be out.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Gentle Mercutio, put thy rapier up.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text: Come, sir, your passado.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
[Draws his sword] Draw, Benvolio. Beat down their
weapons.
Gentlemen, for shame! Forbear this outrage.
Tybalt, Mercutio! The Prince expressly hath
Forbidden bandying in Verona streets.
Hold, Tybalt! Good Mercutio!

sentences.speaker: PETRUCHIO
sentences.text: Away, Tybalt.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
I am hurt.
A plague o’ both your houses! I am sped.
Is he gone and hath nothing?

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: What, art thou hurt?

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Ay, ay, a scratch, a scratch. Marry, ’tis enough.
Where is my page?—Go, villain, fetch a surgeon.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Courage, man. The hurt cannot be much.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Courage, man. The hurt cannot be much.
MERCUTIO’S PAGE exits.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
No, ’tis not so deep as a well nor so wide as a
church-door, but ’tis enough, ’twill serve. Ask for me
tomorrow, and you shall find me a grave man. I am
peppered, I warrant, for this world. A plague o’ both
your houses! Zounds, a dog, a rat, a mouse, a cat to
scratch a man to death! A braggart, a rogue, a villain
that fights by the book of arithmetic! Why the devil
came you between us? I was hurt under your arm.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: I thought all for the best.

sentences.speaker: MERCUTIO
sentences.text:
Help me into some house, Benvolio,
Or I shall faint. A plague o’ both your houses!
They have made worms’ meat of me. I have it,
And soundly too. Your houses!

MERCUTIO and BENVOLIO exit.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
This gentleman, the Prince’s near ally,
My very friend, hath got his mortal hurt
In my behalf. My reputation stained
With Tybalt’s slander. —Tybalt, that an hour
Hath been my kinsman! O sweet Juliet,
Thy beauty hath made me effeminate
And in my temper softened valor’s steel!

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
O Romeo, Romeo, brave Mercutio is dead!
That gallant spirit hath aspired the clouds,
Which too untimely here did scorn the earth.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
O Romeo, Romeo, brave Mercutio is dead!
That gallant spirit hath aspired the clouds,
Which too untimely here did scorn the earth.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
This day’s black fate on more days doth depend.
This but begins the woe others must end.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
This day’s black fate on more days doth depend.
This but begins the woe others must end.
TYBALT enters.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: Here comes the furious Tybalt back again.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Alive in triumph—and Mercutio slain!
Away to heaven, respective lenity,
And fire-eyed fury be my conduct now.
Now, Tybalt, take the “villain” back again
That late thou gavest me, for Mercutio’s soul
Is but a little way above our heads,
Staying for thine to keep him company.
Either thou or I, or both, must go with him.

sentences.text:
Alive in triumph—and Mercutio slain!
Away to heaven, respective lenity,
And fire-eyed fury be my conduct now.
Now, Tybalt, take the “villain” back again
That late thou gavest me, for Mercutio’s soul
Is but a little way above our heads,
Staying for thine to keep him company.
Either thou or I, or both, must go with him.

sentences.speaker: TYBALT
sentences.text:
Thou, wretched boy, that didst consort him here
Shalt with him hence.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: This shall determine that.
They fight. TYBALT falls and dies.
They fight. TYBALT falls and dies.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
Romeo, away, be gone!
The citizens are up, and Tybalt slain.
Stand not amazed. The Prince will doom thee death
If thou art taken. Hence, be gone, away!

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: Why dost thou stay?

sentences.speaker: CITIZEN OF THE WATCH
sentences.text:
Which way ran he that killed Mercutio?
Tybalt, that murderer, which way ran he?

sentences.speaker: CITIZEN OF THE WATCH
sentences.text:
Which way ran he that killed Mercutio?
Tybalt, that murderer, which way ran he?
ROMEO exits.
The CITIZENS OF THE WATCH enter.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text: There lies that Tybalt.

sentences.speaker: CITIZEN OF THE WATCH
sentences.text:
[To TYBALT] Up, sir, go with me.
I charge thee in the Prince’s name, obey.

sentences.speaker: CITIZEN OF THE WATCH
sentences.text:
[To TYBALT] Up, sir, go with me.
I charge thee in the Prince’s name, obey.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE
sentences.text: Where are the vile beginners of this fray?
The PRINCE enters with MONTAGUE, CAPULET, LADY MONTAGUE, LADY CAPULET, and OTHERS.

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
O noble prince, I can discover all
The unlucky manage of this fatal brawl.
There lies the man, slain by young Romeo,
That slew thy kinsman, brave Mercutio.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Tybalt, my cousin! O my brother’s child!
O Prince! O cousin! Husband! Oh, the blood is spilled
Of my dear kinsman! Prince, as thou art true,
For blood of ours shed blood of Montague.
O cousin, cousin!

sentences.speaker: PRINCE
sentences.text: Benvolio, who began this bloody fray?

sentences.speaker: BENVOLIO
sentences.text:
Tybalt here slain, whom Romeo’s hand did slay.
Romeo, that spoke him fair, bade him bethink
How nice the quarrel was and urged withal
Your high displeasure. All this uttered
With gentle breath, calm look, knees humbly bowed,
Could not take truce with the unruly spleen
Of Tybalt deaf to peace, but that he tilts
With piercing steel at bold Mercutio’s breast,
Who, all as hot, turns deadly point to point,
And, with a martial scorn, with one hand beats
Cold death aside and with the other sends
It back to Tybalt, whose dexterity,
Retorts it. Romeo, he cries aloud,
“Hold, friends! Friends, part!” and, swifter than his
tongue,
His agile arm beats down their fatal points,
And ’twixt them rushes—underneath whose arm
An envious thrust from Tybalt hit the life
Of stout Mercutio, and then Tybalt fled.
But by and by comes back to Romeo,
Who had but newly entertained revenge,
And to ’t they go like lightning, for ere I
Could draw to part them was stout Tybalt slain.
And, as he fell, did Romeo turn and fly.
This is the truth, or let Benvolio die.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
He is a kinsman to the Montague.
Affection makes him false. He speaks not true.
Some twenty of them fought in this black strife,
And all those twenty could but kill one life.
I beg for justice, which thou, Prince, must give.
Romeo slew Tybalt. Romeo must not live.

Romeo slew him; he slew Mercutio.
Who now the price of his dear blood doth owe?

sentences.speaker: MONTAGUE
sentences.text:
Not Romeo, Prince, he was Mercutio’s friend.
His fault concludes but what the law should end,
The life of Tybalt.

sentences.speaker: PRINCE
sentences.text:
And for that offense
Immediately we do exile him hence.
I have an interest in your hate's proceeding;
My blood for your rude brawls doth lie a-bleeding.
But I'll amerce you with so strong a fine
That you shall all repent the loss of mine.
I will be deaf to pleading and excuses.
Nor tears nor prayers shall purchase out abuses.
Therefore use none. Let Romeo hence in haste,
Else, when he is found, that hour is his last.
Bear hence this body, and attend our will.
Mercy but murders, pardoning those that kill.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Gallop apace, you fiery-footed steeds,
Toward Phoebus’ lodging. Such a wagoner
As Phaeton would whip you to the west
And bring in cloudy night immediately.
Spread thy close curtain, love-performing night,
That runaways’ eyes may wink, and Romeo
Leap to these arms, untalked of and unseen.
Lovers can see to do their amorous rites
By their own beauties, or, if love be blind,
It best agrees with night. Come, civil night,
Thou sober-suited matron, all in black,
And learn me how to lose a winning match
Played for a pair of stainless maidenhoods.
Hood my unmanned blood bating in my cheeks,
With thy black mantle, till strange love, grow bold,
Think true love acted simple modesty.
Come, night. Come, Romeo. Come, thou day in night,
For thou wilt lie upon the wings of night
Whiter than new snow upon a raven’s back.
Come, gentle night, come, loving, black-browed night,
Give me my Romeo. And when I shall die,
Take him and cut him out in little stars,
And he will make the face of heaven so fine
That all the world will be in love with night
And pay no worship to the garish sun.
Oh, I have bought the mansion of a love,
But not possessed it, and though I am sold,
Not yet enjoyed. So tedious is this day
As is the night before some festival
To an impatient child that hath new robes
And may not wear them.

Spread thy close curtain, love-performing night,
That runaways’ eyes may wink, and Romeo
Leap to these arms, untalked of and unseen.
Lovers can see to do their amorous rites
By their own beauties, or, if love be blind,
It best agrees with night. Come, civil night,
Thou sober-suited matron, all in black,
And learn me how to lose a winning match
Played for a pair of stainless maidenhoods.
Hood my unmanned blood bating in my cheeks,
With thy black mantle, till strange love, grow bold,
Think true love acted simple modesty.
Come, night. Come, Romeo. Come, thou day in night,
For thou wilt lie upon the wings of night
Whiter than new snow upon a raven’s back.
Come, gentle night, come, loving, black-browed night,
Give me my Romeo. And when I shall die,
Take him and cut him out in little stars,
And he will make the face of heaven so fine
That all the world will be in love with night
And pay no worship to the garish sun.
Oh, I have bought the mansion of a love,
But not possessed it, and though I am sold,
Not yet enjoyed. So tedious is this day
As is the night before some festival
To an impatient child that hath new robes
And may not wear them.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Gallop apace, you fiery-footed steeds,
Toward Phoebus’ lodging. Such a wagoner
As Phaeton would whip you to the west
And bring in cloudy night immediately.
Spread thy close curtain, love-performing night,
That runaways’ eyes may wink, and Romeo
Leap to these arms, untalked of and unseen.
Lovers can see to do their amorous rites
By their own beauties, or, if love be blind,
It best agrees with night. Come, civil night,
Thou sober-suited matron, all in black,
And learn me how to lose a winning match
Played for a pair of stainless maidenhoods.
Hood my unmanned blood bating in my cheeks,
With thy black mantle, till strange love, grow bold,
Think true love acted simple modesty.
Come, night. Come, Romeo. Come, thou day in night,
For thou wilt lie upon the wings of night
Whiter than new snow upon a raven’s back.
Come, gentle night, come, loving, black-browed night,
Give me my Romeo. And when I shall die,
Take him and cut him out in little stars,
And he will make the face of heaven so fine
That all the world will be in love with night
And pay no worship to the garish sun.
Oh, I have bought the mansion of a love,
But not possessed it, and though I am sold,
Not yet enjoyed. So tedious is this day
As is the night before some festival
To an impatient child that hath new robes
And may not wear them.
The NURSE enters carrying the rope ladder.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Oh, here comes my Nurse,
And she brings news, and every tongue that speaks
But Romeo’s name speaks heavenly eloquence.—
Now, Nurse, what news? What hast thou there? The cords
That Romeo bid thee fetch?

Ah, weraday! He’s dead, he’s dead, he’s dead!

Alack the day! He’s gone, he’s killed, he’s dead!

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
What devil art thou that dost torment me thus?
This torture should be roared in dismal hell.
Hath Romeo slain himself? Say thou but “ay,”
And that bare vowel I shall poison more
Than the death-darting eye of cockatrice.
I am not I if there be such an I,
Or those eyes shut that makes thee answer “ay.”
If he be slain, say “ay,” or if not, “no.”
Brief sounds determine of my weal or woe.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
I saw the wound, I saw it with mine eyes—
God save the mark!—here on his manly breast.
A piteous corse, a bloody piteous corse.
Pale, pale as ashes, all bedaubed in blood,
All in gore blood. I swoonèd at the sight.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
O, break, my heart, poor bankrupt, break at once!
To prison, eyes, ne’er look on liberty.
Vile earth, to earth resign. End motion here,
And thou and Romeo press one heavy bier.

That ever I should live to see thee dead.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
What storm is this that blows so contrary?
Is Romeo slaughtered, and is Tybalt dead?
My dearest cousin and my dearer lord?
Then, dreadful trumpet, sound the general doom!
For who is living if those two are gone?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
O serpent heart hid with a flowering face!
Did ever dragon keep so fair a cave?
Beautiful tyrant! Fiend angelical!
Dove-feathered raven, wolvish-ravening lamb!
Despisèd substance of divinest show,
Just opposite to what thou justly seem’st.
A damnèd saint, an honorable villain!
O nature, what hadst thou to do in hell
When thou didst bower the spirit of a fiend
In moral paradise of such sweet flesh?
Was ever book containing such vile matter
So fairly bound? Oh, that deceit should dwell
In such a gorgeous palace!

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
There’s no trust,
No faith, no honesty in men. All perjured,
All forsworn, all naught, all dissemblers.
Ah, where’s my man?—Give me some aqua vitae.—
These griefs, these woes, these sorrows make me old.
Shame come to Romeo!

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Blistered be thy tongue
For such a wish! He was not born to shame.
Upon his brow shame is ashamed to sit,
For ’tis a throne where honor may be crowned.
Sole monarch of the universal earth,
Oh, what a beast was I to chide at him!

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Will you speak well of him that killed your cousin?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Shall I speak ill of him that is my husband?
Ah, poor my lord, what tongue shall smooth thy name,
When I, thy three hours’ wife, have mangled it?
But wherefore, villain, didst thou kill my cousin?
That villain cousin would have killed my husband.
Back, foolish tears, back to your native spring.
Your tributary drops belong to woe,
Which you, mistaking, offer up to joy.

Why followed not, when she said “Tybalt’s dead,”
“Thy father” or “thy mother,” nay, or both,
Which modern lamentations might have moved?
But with a rearward following Tybalt’s death,
“Romeo is banishèd.” To speak that word,
Is father, mother, Tybalt, Romeo, Juliet,
All slain, all dead. “Romeo is banishèd.”
There is no end, no limit, measure, bound,
In that word’s death. No words can that woe sound.
Where is my father and my mother, Nurse?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Shall I speak ill of him that is my husband?
Ah, poor my lord, what tongue shall smooth thy name,
When I, thy three hours’ wife, have mangled it?
But wherefore, villain, didst thou kill my cousin?
That villain cousin would have killed my husband.
Back, foolish tears, back to your native spring.
Your tributary drops belong to woe,
Which you, mistaking, offer up to joy.
My husband lives, that Tybalt would have slain,
And Tybalt’s dead, that would have slain my husband.
All this is comfort. Wherefore weep I then?
Some word there was, worser than Tybalt’s death,
That murdered me. I would forget it fain,
But oh, it presses to my memory,
Like damnèd guilty deeds to sinners’ minds.
“Tybalt is dead, and Romeo banishèd.”
That “banishèd,” that one word “banishèd”
Hath slain ten thousand Tybalts. Tybalt’s death
Was woe enough, if it had ended there.
Or, if sour woe delights in fellowship
And needly will be ranked with other griefs,
Why followed not, when she said “Tybalt’s dead,”
“Thy father” or “thy mother,” nay, or both,
Which modern lamentations might have moved?
But with a rearward following Tybalt’s death,
“Romeo is banishèd.” To speak that word,
Is father, mother, Tybalt, Romeo, Juliet,
All slain, all dead. “Romeo is banishèd.”
There is no end, no limit, measure, bound,
In that word’s death. No words can that woe sound.
Where is my father and my mother, Nurse?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Weeping and wailing over Tybalt’s corse.
Will you go to them? I will bring you thither.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Weeping and wailing over Tybalt’s corse.
Will you go to them? I will bring you thither.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Weeping and wailing over Tybalt’s corse.
Will you go to them? I will bring you thither.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Wash they his wounds with tears? Mine shall be spent
When theirs are dry, for Romeo’s banishment.
Take up those cords.—Poor ropes, you are beguiled,
Both you and I, for Romeo is exiled.
He made you for a highway to my bed,
But I, a maid, die maiden-widowèd.
Come, cords.—Come, Nurse. I’ll to my wedding bed.
And death, not Romeo, take my maidenhead!

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Hie to your chamber. I’ll find Romeo
To comfort you. I wot well where he is.
Hark ye, your Romeo will be here at night.
I’ll to him. He is hid at Lawrence’ cell.

sentences.text:
[Giving her a ring] O, find him! Give this ring to my
true knight,
And bid him come to take his last farewell.

FRIAR LAWRENCE enters.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Romeo, come forth. Come forth, thou fearful man.
Affliction is enamoured of thy parts,
And thou art wedded to calamity.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Romeo, come forth. Come forth, thou fearful man.
Affliction is enamoured of thy parts,
And thou art wedded to calamity.

ROMEO enters.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Father, what news? What is the Prince’s doom?
What sorrow craves acquaintance at my hand
That I yet know not?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Father, what news? What is the Prince’s doom?
What sorrow craves acquaintance at my hand
That I yet know not?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Father, what news? What is the Prince’s doom?
What sorrow craves acquaintance at my hand
That I yet know not?

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Too familiar
Is my dear son with such sour company.
I bring thee tidings of the Prince’s doom.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Too familiar
Is my dear son with such sour company.
I bring thee tidings of the Prince’s doom.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: What less than doomsday is the Prince’s doom?

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
A gentler judgment vanished from his lips:
Not body’s death, but body’s banishment.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
A gentler judgment vanished from his lips:
Not body’s death, but body’s banishment.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Ha, banishment! Be merciful, say “death,”
For exile hath more terror in his look,
Much more than death. Do not say “banishment.”

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:

For exile hath more terror in his look,

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Hence from Verona art thou banishèd.
Be patient, for the world is broad and wide.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Hence from Verona art thou banishèd.
Be patient, for the world is broad and wide.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
There is no world without Verona walls
But purgatory, torture, hell itself.
Hence “banishèd” is banished from the world,
And world’s exile is death. Then “banishèd,”
Is death mistermed. Calling death “banishment,”
Thou cutt’st my head off with a golden ax
And smilest upon the stroke that murders me.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
O deadly sin! O rude unthankfulness!
Thy fault our law calls death, but the kind Prince,
Taking thy part, hath rushed aside the law,
And turned that black word “death” to “banishment.”
This is dear mercy, and thou seest it not.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
‘Tis torture and not mercy. Heaven is here,
Where Juliet lives, and every cat and dog
And little mouse, every unworthy thing,
Live here in heaven and may look on her,
But Romeo may not. More validity,
More honorable state, more courtship lives
In carrion flies than Romeo. They may seize
On the white wonder of dear Juliet’s hand
And steal immortal blessing from her lips,
Who even in pure and vestal modesty,
Still blush, as thinking their own kisses sin.
But Romeo may not. He is banishèd.
Flies may do this, but I from this must fly.
They are free men, but I am banishèd.
And sayst thou yet that exile is not death?
Hadst thou no poison mixed, no sharp-ground knife,
No sudden mean of death, though ne’er so mean,
But “banishèd” to kill me?—“Banishèd”!
O Friar, the damnèd use that word in hell.
Howling attends it. How hast thou the heart,
Being a divine, a ghostly confessor,
A sin-absolver, and my friend professed,
To mangle me with that word “banishèd”?

Live here in heaven and may look on her,
But Romeo may not. More validity,
More honorable state, more courtship lives
In carrion flies than Romeo. They may seize
On the white wonder of dear Juliet’s hand
And steal immortal blessing from her lips,
Who even in pure and vestal modesty,
Still blush, as thinking their own kisses sin.
But Romeo may not. He is banishèd.
Flies may do this, but I from this must fly.
They are free men, but I am banishèd.
And sayst thou yet that exile is not death?
Hadst thou no poison mixed, no sharp-ground knife,
No sudden mean of death, though ne’er so mean,
But “banishèd” to kill me?—“Banishèd”!
O Friar, the damnèd use that word in hell.
Howling attends it. How hast thou the heart,
Being a divine, a ghostly confessor,
A sin-absolver, and my friend professed,
To mangle me with that word “banishèd”?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
‘Tis torture and not mercy. Heaven is here,
Where Juliet lives, and every cat and dog
And little mouse, every unworthy thing,
Live here in heaven and may look on her,
But Romeo may not. More validity,
More honorable state, more courtship lives
In carrion flies than Romeo. They may seize
On the white wonder of dear Juliet’s hand
And steal immortal blessing from her lips,
Who even in pure and vestal modesty,
Still blush, as thinking their own kisses sin.
But Romeo may not. He is banishèd.
Flies may do this, but I from this must fly.
They are free men, but I am banishèd.
And sayst thou yet that exile is not death?
Hadst thou no poison mixed, no sharp-ground knife,
No sudden mean of death, though ne’er so mean,
But “banishèd” to kill me?—“Banishèd”!
O Friar, the damnèd use that word in hell.
Howling attends it. How hast thou the heart,
Being a divine, a ghostly confessor,
A sin-absolver, and my friend professed,
To mangle me with that word “banishèd”?

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: Thou fond mad man, hear me a little speak.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Oh, thou wilt speak again of banishment.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
I’ll give thee armor to keep off that word—
Adversity’s sweet milk, philosophy—
To comfort thee though thou art banishèd.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Yet “banishèd”? Hang up philosophy!
Unless philosophy can make a Juliet,
Displant a town, reverse a prince’s doom,
It helps not, it prevails not. Talk no more.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: Oh, then I see that madmen have no ears.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: How should they, when that wise men have no eyes?

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: Let me dispute with thee of thy estate.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Thou canst not speak of that thou dost not feel.
Wert thou as young as I, Juliet thy love,
An hour but married, Tybalt murderèd,
Doting like me, and like me banishèd,
Then mightst thou speak, then mightst thou tear thy
hair
And fall upon the ground, as I do now,
Taking the measure of an unmade grave.

sentences.text: Arise. One knocks. Good Romeo, hide thyself.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: Arise. One knocks. Good Romeo, hide thyself.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: Arise. One knocks. Good Romeo, hide thyself.
Knocking sounds from offstage.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Not I, unless the breath of heartsick groans,
Mistlike, infold me from the search of eyes.
Knocking

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Hark, how they knock!—Who’s there?—Romeo, arise.
Thou wilt be taken.—Stay awhile.—Stand up.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:

sentences.text:
Run to my study.—By and by!—God’s will,
What simpleness is this!—I come, I come.

sentences.text: Who knocks so hard? Whence come you? What’s your will?

sentences.text:

errand.
I come from Lady Juliet.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: [Opens the door] Welcome then.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
O holy Friar, O, tell me, holy Friar,
Where is my lady’s lord? Where’s Romeo?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
O holy Friar, O, tell me, holy Friar,
Where is my lady’s lord? Where’s Romeo?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
O holy Friar, O, tell me, holy Friar,
Where is my lady’s lord? Where’s Romeo?
The NURSE enters.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text: There on the ground, with his own tears made drunk.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Oh, he is even in my mistress’ case,
Just in her case. O woeful sympathy,
Piteous predicament! Even so lies she,
Blubbering and weeping, weeping and blubbering.
Stand up, stand up. Stand, an you be a man.
For Juliet’s sake, for her sake, rise and stand.
Why should you fall into so deep an O?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Nurse!

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Ah sir, ah sir. Death’s the end of all.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Ah sir, ah sir. Death’s the end of all.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Spakest thou of Juliet? How is it with her?
Doth she not think me an old murderer,
Now I have stained the childhood of our joy
With blood removed but little from her own?
Where is she? And how doth she? And what says
My concealed lady to our canceled love?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Oh, she says nothing, sir, but weeps and weeps,
And now falls on her bed, and then starts up,
And “Tybalt” calls, and then on Romeo cries,
And then down falls again.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
As if that name,
Shot from the deadly level of a gun,
Did murder her, as that name’s cursed hand
Murdered her kinsman. O, tell me, Friar, tell me,
In what vile part of this anatomy
Doth my name lodge? Tell me, that I may sack
The hateful mansion. [He draws his dagger]

sentences.text:

The hateful mansion. [He draws his dagger]

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Hold thy desperate hand.
Art thou a man? Thy form cries out thou art.
Thy tears are womanish. Thy wild acts denote
The unreasonable fury of a beast.
Unseemly woman in a seeming man,
And ill-beseeming beast in seeming both!
Thou hast amazed me. By my holy order,
I thought thy disposition better tempered.
Hast thou slain Tybalt? Wilt thou slay thyself,
And slay thy lady that in thy life lives
By doing damnèd hate upon thyself?
Why rail’st thou on thy birth, the heaven, and earth?
Since birth and heaven and earth, all three do meet
In thee at once, which thou at once wouldst lose?
Fie, fie, thou shamest thy shape, thy love, thy wit,
Which, like a usurer, abound’st in all
And usest none in that true use indeed
Which should bedeck thy shape, thy love, thy wit.
Thy noble shape is but a form of wax,
Digressing from the valor of a man;
Thy dear love sworn but hollow perjury,
Killing that love which thou hast vowed to cherish;
Thy wit, that ornament to shape and love,
Misshapen in the conduct of them both,
Like powder in a skill-less soldier’s flask,
Is set afire by thine own ignorance;
And thou dismembered with thine own defence.
What, rouse thee, man! Thy Juliet is alive,
For whose dear sake thou wast but lately dead—
There art thou happy. Tybalt would kill thee,
But thou slew’st Tybalt—there art thou happy.
The law that threatened death becomes thy friend
And turns it to exile—there art thou happy.
A pack of blessings light upon thy back,
Happiness courts thee in her best array,
But, like a misbehaved and sullen wench,
Thou pout’st upon thy fortune and thy love.
Take heed, take heed, for such die miserable.
Go, get thee to thy love, as was decreed.
Ascend her chamber, hence, and comfort her.
But look thou stay not till the watch be set,
For then thou canst not pass to Mantua,
Where thou shalt live, till we can find a time
To blaze your marriage, reconcile your friends,
Beg pardon of the Prince, and call thee back
With twenty hundred thousand times more joy
Than thou went’st forth in lamentation.—
Go before, Nurse. Commend me to thy lady,
And bid her hasten all the house to bed,
Which heavy sorrow makes them apt unto.
Romeo is coming.

Thou hast amazed me. By my holy order,
I thought thy disposition better tempered.
Hast thou slain Tybalt? Wilt thou slay thyself,
And slay thy lady that in thy life lives
By doing damnèd hate upon thyself?
Why rail’st thou on thy birth, the heaven, and earth?
Since birth and heaven and earth, all three do meet
In thee at once, which thou at once wouldst lose?
Fie, fie, thou shamest thy shape, thy love, thy wit,
Which, like a usurer, abound’st in all
And usest none in that true use indeed
Which should bedeck thy shape, thy love, thy wit.
Thy noble shape is but a form of wax,
Digressing from the valor of a man;
Thy dear love sworn but hollow perjury,
Killing that love which thou hast vowed to cherish;
Thy wit, that ornament to shape and love,
Misshapen in the conduct of them both,
Like powder in a skill-less soldier’s flask,
Is set afire by thine own ignorance;
And thou dismembered with thine own defence.
What, rouse thee, man! Thy Juliet is alive,
For whose dear sake thou wast but lately dead—
There art thou happy. Tybalt would kill thee,
But thou slew’st Tybalt—there art thou happy.
The law that threatened death becomes thy friend
And turns it to exile—there art thou happy.
A pack of blessings light upon thy back,
Happiness courts thee in her best array,
But, like a misbehaved and sullen wench,
Thou pout’st upon thy fortune and thy love.
Take heed, take heed, for such die miserable.
Go, get thee to thy love, as was decreed.
Ascend her chamber, hence, and comfort her.
But look thou stay not till the watch be set,
For then thou canst not pass to Mantua,
Where thou shalt live, till we can find a time
To blaze your marriage, reconcile your friends,
Beg pardon of the Prince, and call thee back
With twenty hundred thousand times more joy
Than thou went’st forth in lamentation.—
Go before, Nurse. Commend me to thy lady,
And bid her hasten all the house to bed,
Which heavy sorrow makes them apt unto.
Romeo is coming.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
O Lord, I could have stayed here all the night
To hear good counsel. Oh, what learning is!
My lord, I’ll tell my lady you will come.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
O Lord, I could have stayed here all the night
To hear good counsel. Oh, what learning is!
My lord, I’ll tell my lady you will come.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Do so, and bid my sweet prepare to chide.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Here, sir, a ring she bid me give you, sir.
[Gives ROMEO JULIET’s ring]
Hie you, make haste, for it grows very late.
Exit NURSE

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: How well my comfort is revived by this!

sentences.speaker: FRIAR LAWRENCE
sentences.text:
Go hence. Good night. And here stands all your state:
Either be gone before the watch be set,
Or by the break of day disguised from hence.
Sojourn in Mantua. I’ll find out your man,
And he shall signify from time to time
Every good hap to you that chances here.
Give me thy hand. ‘Tis late. Farewell, good night.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
But that a joy past joy calls out on me,
It were a grief so brief to part with thee.
Farewell.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
But that a joy past joy calls out on me,
It were a grief so brief to part with thee.
Farewell.
They all exit.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Things have fall’n out, sir, so unluckily,
That we have had no time to move our daughter.
Look you, she loved her kinsman Tybalt dearly,
And so did I. Well, we were born to die.
‘Tis very late. She’ll not come down tonight.
I promise you, but for your company,
I would have been abed an hour ago.

Enter CAPULET, LADY CAPULET, and PARIS.

sentences.speaker: PARIS
sentences.text:
These times of woe afford no time to woo.
Madam, good night. Commend me to your daughter.

sentences.speaker: PARIS
sentences.text:
These times of woe afford no time to woo.
Madam, good night. Commend me to your daughter.

sentences.speaker: PARIS
sentences.text:
These times of woe afford no time to woo.
Madam, good night. Commend me to your daughter.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
I will, and know her mind early tomorrow.
Tonight she is mewed up to her heaviness.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
I will, and know her mind early tomorrow.
Tonight she is mewed up to her heaviness.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
I will, and know her mind early tomorrow.
Tonight she is mewed up to her heaviness.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Sir Paris, I will make a desperate tender
Of my child’s love. I think she will be ruled
In all respects by me. Nay, more, I doubt it not.—
Wife, go you to her ere you go to bed.
Acquaint her here of my son Paris’ love,
And bid her, mark you me, on Wednesday next—
But, soft! What day is this?

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Sir Paris, I will make a desperate tender
Of my child’s love. I think she will be ruled
In all respects by me. Nay, more, I doubt it not.—
Wife, go you to her ere you go to bed.
Acquaint her here of my son Paris’ love,
And bid her, mark you me, on Wednesday next—
But, soft! What day is this?

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Sir Paris, I will make a desperate tender
Of my child’s love. I think she will be ruled
In all respects by me. Nay, more, I doubt it not.—
Wife, go you to her ere you go to bed.

sentences.speaker: PARIS
sentences.text: Monday, my lord.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Monday! Ha, ha. Well, Wednesday is too soon,
O’ Thursday let it be.—O’ Thursday, tell her,
She shall be married to this noble earl.—
Will you be ready? Do you like this haste?
We’ll keep no great ado, a friend or two.
For, hark you, Tybalt being slain so late,
It may be thought we held him carelessly,
Being our kinsman, if we revel much.
Therefore we’ll have some half a dozen friends,
And there an end. But what say you to Thursday?

sentences.speaker: PARIS
sentences.text: My lord, I would that Thursday were tomorrow.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Well, get you gone. O' Thursday be it then.
[To his wife] Go you to Juliet ere you go to bed.
Prepare her, wife, against this wedding day.—
Farewell, my lord.—Light to my chamber, ho!—
Afore me, it is so very late that we
May call it early by and by. Good night.

They all exit.
ROMEO and JULIET enter above the stage.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Wilt thou be gone? It is not yet near day.
It was the nightingale, and not the lark,
That pierced the fearful hollow of thine ear.
Nightly she sings on yon pomegranate tree.
Believe me, love, it was the nightingale.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
It was the lark, the herald of the morn,
No nightingale. Look, love, what envious streaks
Do lace the severing clouds in yonder east.
Night’s candles are burnt out, and jocund day
Stands tiptoe on the misty mountain tops.
I must be gone and live, or stay and die.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Yon light is not daylight, I know it, I.
It is some meteor that the sun exhales
To be to thee this night a torchbearer,
And light thee on thy way to Mantua.
Therefore stay yet. Thou need’st not to be gone.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Let me be ta’en. Let me be put to death.
I am content, so thou wilt have it so.
I’ll say yon grey is not the morning’s eye.
‘Tis but the pale reflex of Cynthia’s brow.
Nor that is not the lark, whose notes do beat
The vaulty heaven so high above our heads.
I have more care to stay than will to go.
Come, death, and welcome! Juliet wills it so.—
How is ’t, my soul? Let’s talk. It is not day.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
It is, it is. Hie hence! Be gone, away!
It is the lark that sings so out of tune,
Straining harsh discords and unpleasing sharps.
Some say the lark makes sweet division.
This doth not so, for she divideth us.
Some say the lark and loathèd toad change eyes.
Oh, now I would they had changed voices too,
Since arm from arm that voice doth us affray,
Hunting thee hence with hunt’s-up to the day.
O, now be gone. More light and light it grows.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: More light and light, more dark and dark our woes!
The NURSE enters.

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text: Madam.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Nurse?

sentences.speaker: NURSE
sentences.text:
Your lady mother is coming to your chamber.
The day is broke. Be wary, look about.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Then, window, let day in and let life out.
The NURSE exits.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Farewell, farewell. One kiss, and I’ll descend.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text: Farewell, farewell. One kiss, and I’ll descend.
They kiss. ROMEO goes down.
They kiss. ROMEO goes down.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Art thou gone so, love, lord? Ay, husband, friend,
I must hear from thee every day in the hour,
For in a minute there are many days.
Oh, by this count I shall be much in years
Ere I again behold my Romeo.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Farewell!
I will omit no opportunity
That may convey my greetings, love, to thee.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
Farewell!
I will omit no opportunity
That may convey my greetings, love, to thee.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Oh, think’st thou we shall ever meet again?

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
I doubt it not, and all these woes shall serve
For sweet discourses in our time to come.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
I doubt it not, and all these woes shall serve
For sweet discourses in our time to come.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
O God, I have an ill-divining soul.
Methinks I see thee now, thou art so low
As one dead in the bottom of a tomb.
Either my eyesight fails, or thou look’st pale.

sentences.speaker: ROMEO
sentences.text:
And trust me, love, in my eye so do you.
Dry sorrow drinks our blood. Adieu, adieu!

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
O Fortune, Fortune! All men call thee fickle.
If thou art fickle, what dost thou with him
That is renowned for faith? Be fickle, fortune,
For then, I hope, thou wilt not keep him long,
But send him back.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text: [From within] Ho, daughter, are you up?

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text: [From within] Ho, daughter, are you up?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Who is ’t that calls? Is it my lady mother?
Is she not down so late or up so early?
What unaccustomed cause procures her hither?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:

Is she not down so late or up so early?
What unaccustomed cause procures her hither?

LADY CAPULET enters.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text: Why, how now, Juliet?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Madam, I am not well.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Evermore weeping for your cousin’s death?
What, wilt thou wash him from his grave with tears?
An if thou couldst, thou couldst not make him live.
Therefore, have done. Some grief shows much of love,
But much of grief shows still some want of wit.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Yet let me weep for such a feeling loss.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
So shall you feel the loss, but not the friend
Which you weep for.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Feeling so the loss,
Cannot choose but ever weep the friend.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Well, girl, thou weep’st not so much for his death,
As that the villain lives which slaughtered him.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: What villain, madam?

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text: That same villain, Romeo.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
[Aside] Villain and he be many miles asunder.
[To LADY CAPULET] God pardon him! I do, with all my
heart,
And yet no man like he doth grieve my heart.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text: That is because the traitor murderer lives.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Ay, madam, from the reach of these my hands.
Would none but I might venge my cousin’s death!

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Ay, madam, from the reach of these my hands.
Would none but I might venge my cousin’s death!

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
We will have vengeance for it, fear thou not.
Then weep no more. I’ll send to one in Mantua,
Where that same banished runagate doth live,
Shall give him such an unaccustomed dram
That he shall soon keep Tybalt company.
And then, I hope, thou wilt be satisfied.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Indeed, I never shall be satisfied
With Romeo, till I behold him—dead—
Is my poor heart for a kinsman vexed.
Madam, if you could find out but a man
To bear a poison, I would temper it,
That Romeo should, upon receipt thereof,
Soon sleep in quiet. Oh, how my heart abhors
To hear him named, and cannot come to him.
To wreak the love I bore my cousin
Upon his body that slaughtered him!

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Find thou the means, and I’ll find such a man.
But now I’ll tell thee joyful tidings, girl.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Find thou the means, and I’ll find such a man.
But now I’ll tell thee joyful tidings, girl.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
And joy comes well in such a needy time.
What are they, beseech your ladyship?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
And joy comes well in such a needy time.
What are they, beseech your ladyship?

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Well, well, thou hast a careful father, child.
One who, to put thee from thy heaviness,
Hath sorted out a sudden day of joy
That thou expect’st not, nor I looked not for.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Well, well, thou hast a careful father, child.
One who, to put thee from thy heaviness,
Hath sorted out a sudden day of joy
That thou expect’st not, nor I looked not for.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text: Madam, in happy time, what day is that?

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Marry, my child, early next Thursday morn,
The gallant, young, and noble gentleman,
The County Paris, at Saint Peter’s Church,
Shall happily make thee there a joyful bride.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Now, by Saint Peter’s Church and Peter too,
He shall not make me there a joyful bride.
I wonder at this haste, that I must wed
Ere he, that should be husband, comes to woo.
I pray you, tell my lord and father, madam,
I will not marry yet. And when I do, I swear
It shall be Romeo, whom you know I hate,
Rather than Paris. These are news indeed!

CAPULET and the NURSE enter.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Here comes your father. Tell him so yourself,
And see how he will take it at your hands.

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:
Here comes your father. Tell him so yourself,
And see how he will take it at your hands.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
When the sun sets the air doth drizzle dew,
But for the sunset of my brother’s son
It rains downright.
How now? A conduit, girl? What, still in tears,
Evermore showering? In one little body
Thou counterfeit’st a bark, a sea, a wind,
For still thy eyes, which I may call the sea,
Do ebb and flow with tears. The bark thy body is,
Sailing in this salt flood. The winds thy sighs,
Who, raging with thy tears, and they with them,
Without a sudden calm will overset
Thy tempest-tossèd body. —How now, wife?
Have you delivered to her our decree?

sentences.text:
Ay, sir, but she will none, she gives you thanks.
I would the fool were married to her grave!

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:

I would the fool were married to her grave!

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text:

I would the fool were married to her grave!

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Soft, take me with you, take me with you, wife.
How, will she none? Doth she not give us thanks?
Is she not proud? Doth she not count her blessed,
Unworthy as she is, that we have wrought
So worthy a gentleman to be her bride?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Not proud you have, but thankful that you have.
Proud can I never be of what I hate,
But thankful even for hate that is meant love.

But thankful even for hate that is meant love.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
How, how, how, how? Chopped logic! What is this?
“Proud,” and “I thank you,” and “I thank you not,”
And yet “not proud”? Mistress minion you,
Thank me no thankings, nor proud me no prouds,
But fettle your fine joints ‘gainst Thursday next
To go with Paris to Saint Peter’s Church,
Or I will drag thee on a hurdle thither.
Out, you green sickness, carrion! Out, you baggage!
You tallow face!

sentences.text: Fie, fie! What, are you mad?

sentences.speaker: LADY CAPULET
sentences.text: Fie, fie! What, are you mad?

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Good Father, I beseech you on my knees,
Hear me with patience but to speak a word.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
Hang thee, young baggage! Disobedient wretch!
I tell thee what: get thee to church o’ Thursday,
Or never after look me in the face.
Speak not. Reply not. Do not answer me.
My fingers itch.—Wife, we scarce thought us blest
That God had lent us but this only child,
But now I see this one is one too much
And that we have a curse in having her.
Out on her, hilding!

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
God’s bread! It makes me mad.
Day, night, hour, tide, time, work, play,
Alone, in company, still my care hath been
To have her matched. And having now provided
A gentleman of noble parentage,
Of fair demesnes, youthful, and nobly trained,
Stuffed, as they say, with honorable parts,
Proportioned as one’s thought would wish a man—
And then to have a wretched puling fool,
A whining mammet, in her fortune’s tender,
To answer “I’ll not wed,” “I cannot love,”
“I am too young,” “I pray you, pardon me.”—
But, an you will not wed, I’ll pardon you.
Graze where you will, you shall not house with me.
Look to ’t, think on ’t, I do not use to jest.
Thursday is near. Lay hand on heart, advise.
An you be mine, I’ll give you to my friend.
An you be not, hang, beg, starve, die in the streets,
For, by my soul, I’ll ne’er acknowledge thee,
Nor what is mine shall never do thee good.
Trust to ’t, bethink you. I’ll not be forsworn.

Thursday is near. Lay hand on heart, advise.
An you be mine, I’ll give you to my friend.
An you be not, hang, beg, starve, die in the streets,
For, by my soul, I’ll ne’er acknowledge thee,
Nor what is mine shall never do thee good.
Trust to ’t, bethink you. I’ll not be forsworn.

sentences.speaker: CAPULET
sentences.text:
God’s bread! It makes me mad.
Day, night, hour, tide, time, work, play,
Alone, in company, still my care hath been
To have her matched. And having now provided
A gentleman of noble parentage,
Of fair demesnes, youthful, and nobly trained,
Stuffed, as they say, with honorable parts,
Proportioned as one’s thought would wish a man—
And then to have a wretched puling fool,
A whining mammet, in her fortune’s tender,
To answer “I’ll not wed,” “I cannot love,”
“I am too young,” “I pray you, pardon me.”—
But, an you will not wed, I’ll pardon you.
Graze where you will, you shall not house with me.
Look to ’t, think on ’t, I do not use to jest.
Thursday is near. Lay hand on heart, advise.
An you be mine, I’ll give you to my friend.
An you be not, hang, beg, starve, die in the streets,
For, by my soul, I’ll ne’er acknowledge thee,
Nor what is mine shall never do thee good.
Trust to ’t, bethink you. I’ll not be forsworn.

sentences.speaker: JULIET
sentences.text:
Is there no pity sitting in the clouds
That sees into the bottom of my grief?—
O sweet my mother, cast me not away!
Delay this marriage for a month, a week.
Or, if you do not, make the bridal bed
In that dim monument where Tybalt lies.
